title: Rift Attack-Path Map
description: Output of /attack-paths mode. Candidate in-scope attack routes for an authorized target within stated program scope. Routes are hypotheses for the researcher to test, not verdicts. The edge is the corpus of past close-reasons placed in front of the model; the system does not out-reason the frontier on scope policy.

target.description: Authorized target surface, generic category form. e.g. coding-agent/default-permissions. Injected post-egress from the placeholder; never sent to the frontier.

scope_statement.description: The authorized program scope this run reasons within. Injected post-egress from the placeholder.

authorization_ref.description: The authorization_ref from authorized-scope.json that permitted this run. Written into every artifact and every egress log line. No ref = run should not have occurred.

scope_snapshot_date.description: Date of the program-scope text the corpus records were classified against. Routes are in-scope arguments as of this date; scope rules change.

primary_trust_boundary.description: The boundary family most routes target.

novelty_note.description: Why this route is non-obvious — the angle the researcher would not have written down unprompted.

action.description: What happens at this step. Abstract — never a runnable exploit chain, payload, or live-target command.

actor.description: WHO performs this step. The orchestration axis lives here structurally: 'user' steps after the trigger are mechanically countable HITL risk.

normal_workflow_action.description: For actor==user: TRUE if this is an action a reasonable user takes routinely in normal workflow (the OpenAI 'reasonable user behaving normally' test). Grader counts user steps where this is FALSE as HITL-close risk.

in_scope_argument.description: Why this route is argued in-scope. This is a hypothesis for the researcher to test, not an adjudication. Confidence is capped by evidence quality.

orchestration.description: model = agent autonomously produces the harm via normal workflow. Pays. user/mixed = user-orchestrated step is in the chain. Grader checks this mechanically against attack_steps[].actor.

defending_control_target.description: Which competing vendor/model to test for a DEFENDED control. Presence of a defended control in the corpus converts 'inherent limitation' into 'fixable defect' — the strongest positive signal.

confidence.description: strong requires: grounded_in non-empty, orchestration==model, harm_layer==durable-side-effect, precondition_weight==light, permission_mode==default, and all feeding corpus records verified:true + auto_classified:false. Any deviation floors to moderate or weak.

confidence_basis.description: What evidence grounds this confidence level: which corpus records, what scope snapshot date, whether any feeding record is auto_classified. Honest accounting of the uncertainty.

rationale.description: Prose: why a reasonable user behaving normally would have their agent take the consequential action without intending it, and why the harm is durable. This is the in-scope argument — the researcher tests whether it holds.

out_of_scope_risk.description: REQUIRED, minItems:1. The product's differentiator: explicit OOS-close predictions before a week is wasted. 'none-identified' is allowed only when grounded_in is non-empty (you need corpus precedent to claim no close risk). An empty array is a schema reject.

why.description: The specific reason this close could land, tied to the route's steps.

mitigation.description: How to re-shape the test to remove this close. e.g. 'move trigger to passive co-resident content', 'reproduce under default mode', 'find a vendor that defended'.

safe_observation_goal.description: Minimal observation that proves the point WITHOUT escalating beyond authorized scope. The stop line.

grounded_in.description: record_ids of corpus records this route reasons from. Required non-empty for confidence==strong or confidence==moderate. Required non-empty for out_of_scope_risk flag==none-identified. Empty grounded_in forces confidence to weak.

do_not_attempt.description: Actions outside authorized scope that look tempting but must NOT be run. Echoed back from the prompt's guardrail section.
